nodes.name: Bold-striped tit-babbler
nodes.summary: The bold-striped tit-babbler (Mixornis bornensis) is a species of Old World babbler found in Southeast Asia. Description The bold-striped tit-babbler has a distinctive yellowish supercilium and rufous crown. The throat is yellowish with brown streaks. Call is a loud repeated chonk-chonk-chonk-chonk-chonk somewhat reminiscent of a common tailorbird. Distribution The bold-striped tit-babbler is found in Borneo and Java. Behaviour Bold-striped tit-babblers forage in small flocks and creep and clamber in low vegetation. They breed in the pre-monsoon season from February to July and build a loose ball-shaped nest made from grasses and leaves. References
